question: What is the primary difference between a health savings account (HSA) and a flexible spending account (FSA)?
options: HSAs are portable and have no use-it-or-lose-it rule
answer: HSAs are portable and have no use-it-or-lose-it rule
explanation: HSAs are portable and allow funds to roll over year to year, while FSAs have a use-it-or-lose-it provision.
